title: What if Shinji had Battled the MPEs
display_title: What if Shinji had Battled the MPEs
wikitext:
[]: What if Shinji had Battled the MPE's             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Wed Apr 01, 2015 4:50 pm Having watched the end of EoE, I think I can say with confidence that the Mass Production EVA's would not have defeated Unit 02 if Shinji and Unit 01 had been available to provide assistance. But that begs the question--suppose that had happened, and SEELE's plan was thwarted. How would that alter the course of the story?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Apr 01, 2015 5:08 pm It's Eva. If Shinji got up there, some contrivance would rob him of power or he'd get pinned down while the harpies rapemurder Asuka, and then Yui would hulk out, because the psychotic robot mom doesn't want her precious boy invading her Sudetenland, if you know what I mean.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Wed Apr 01, 2015 6:04 pm I think the story would have been unaffected, because Gendo and Rei (the primary causes of the impact) were already on their way to Lilith. Regardless of what happened on the surface, Rei would still have rebelled against Gendo at the last minute and handed control of the impact to Shinji.

[]: Re: What if Shinji had Battled the MPE's             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Wed Apr 01, 2015 6:27 pm El Squibbonator wrote: Having watched the end of EoE, I think I can say with confidence that the Mass Production Robot's would not have defeated Unit 02 if Shinji and Unit 01 had been available to provide assistance. But that begs the question--suppose that had happened, and SEELE's plan was thwarted. How would that alter the course of the story?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 01, 2015 6:31 pm NGE2 answers these questions. If Shinji (Scenario 1) fails to defeat MP Evas, EoE events ( Bad End) happens. If he succeeds in stopping them, Rei rejects Gendo, but checks on Shinji instead of returning to Lilith (Good End, though Kaji & Misato are dead, since EoE. Asuka & others alive, just not shown). Most likely in EoE proper, it still would have occurred since various NGE dialogues state Third Impact was inevitable (Hence Yui trying to turn a horrible situation into a positive via humanity briefly riding out 3I). Edit: Minor typos.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Wed Apr 01, 2015 6:41 pm That was what I assumed, but I was asking because I wondered whether it would be any different than in the manga, where, if you recall, he does end up doing this.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Apr 01, 2015 6:46 pm I consider any end where Instrumentality doesn't happen bad end.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Wed Apr 01, 2015 7:07 pm Chuckman wrote: I consider any end where Instrumentality doesn't happen bad end.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Apr 01, 2015 7:16 pm LordThaeon wrote: How come? Visuals and the mindscrew factor aside, what would the audience miss if Anno had decided to have Instrumentality be averted? The themes of the Black Orange Juice Egg are already shown, displayed, analyzed and discussed many times in the series proper. So what's one or more key factor that makes Instrumentality vital for the story?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Apr 02, 2015 2:57 am Also it means humans still have Adam, Lilith, and 1+ Evas to be screwing around with, meaning SEELE can still try to get their way without a benevolent goddess intervening.

[]:             Quote Post by Cosmo11 » Thu Apr 02, 2015 5:09 am Also, it means that everyone who died prior to Instrumentality will stay dead.

[]: Re: What if Shinji had Battled the MPE's             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Apr 02, 2015 3:28 pm El Squibbonator wrote: How would that alter the course of the story?

[]: Re: What if Shinji had Battled the MPE's             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 02, 2015 4:24 pm El Squibbonator wrote: Having watched the end of EoE, I think I can say with confidence that the Mass Production EVA's would not have defeated Unit 02 if Shinji and Unit 01 had been available to provide assistance. But that begs the question--suppose that had happened, and SEELE's plan was thwarted. How would that alter the course of the story?

[]: Re: What if Shinji had Battled the MPE's             Quote Post by Lavinius » Thu Apr 02, 2015 4:42 pm Bagheera wrote: As long as the Evas exist, they are a threat to mankind. This is why, to my mind, Yui's grand plan was a crazy Xanatos Gambit that began with building the Evas and figuring out how they worked, advanced to destroying the Angels, and ended with the Evas destroyed. Hence her allowing the MPEs to destroy Unit 02, having Lilith take care of the MPEs, and then finally leaving Earth in the only remaining Eva. I can't prove this, of course, but it's the only setup I've seen thus far that doesn't paint her as batshit insane.

[]: Re: What if Shinji had Battled the MPE's             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 02, 2015 4:48 pm Lavinius wrote: This kind of seems to contradict something I think I've seen you say in the past, that the post-Third Impact won't be so bad in part since Lilim knows how to build the S2 engines. Obviously an S2 engine could easily be engineered into... making? powering? an Eva or Angel or some such equivalent, so unless Yui destroyed that knowledge also that would be kind of pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Apr 02, 2015 8:31 pm The question put to me has been answered in great detail but I will add this: 1. Others may disagree with me on this point but besides the immediate casualties of EoE 3I created an afterlife for everyone. 2. The aftermath of 3I is a moment of great personal apotheosis for Asuka. Again others disagree with me on this point. 3. 3I is inevitable, because ~*@ F i f t h D i m e n s i o n @*~ Therefore any end that turns away from it is a bad end (and does not exist because there is no Rei-Lilith to observe it anyway).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 02, 2015 8:42 pm Chuckman wrote: 2. The aftermath of 3I is a moment of great personal apotheosis for Asuka. Again others disagree with me on this point.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Apr 02, 2015 8:58 pm Chuckman wrote: 3. 3I is inevitable, because ~*@ F i f t h D i m e n s i o n @*~ .

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Apr 02, 2015 9:42 pm Not necessarily. Anno et. al. aren't really bound to use the same metaphysics. The Bookend Rei may simply be a red herring. Then again, what would Eva be without Instrumentality? It's a matter of theme more than mechanics, truly. @Bagheera: As I recall, we still disagree as to the nature of that apotheosis.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Thu Apr 02, 2015 10:03 pm Chuckman wrote: Not necessarily. Anno et. al. aren't really bound to use the same metaphysics. The Bookend Rei may simply be a red herring. Then again, what would Eva be without Instrumentality? It's a matter of theme more than mechanics, truly. @Bagheera: As I recall, we still disagree as to the nature of that apotheosis.

But that begs the question--suppose that had happened, and SEELE's plan was thwarted. How would that alter the course of the story?
